note: Authoritative dataset registry. INVARIANTS: (1) `uid` is stable, opaque, and NEVER shown as an address; (2) a `uid` maps to exactly one dataset and is NEVER reused; (3) a retired slug is NEVER re-pointed at a different dataset. The human-friendly address is `<schema>.<name>` (self-documenting: source.* = raw ingested data, crosswalk.* = derived joins). `name` is the SQL-legal leaf, the web URL slug, and the R2 prefix leaf. On rename: keep `uid`, move the old slug into `aliases`, emit a 301 (web) + a compatibility VIEW at the old name (SQL). Data VERSIONS are a separate axis, handled by the content-addressed `<sha>.parquet` + manifest pointer. Each entry also carries a `protocol` pointer to its arcform.yaml build recipe (how it is made) — null until authored.

schemas.source: Raw ingested reference data, one namespace per what-it-is.
schemas.crosswalk: Derived joins between datasets — the materialised relationships (the product).
